intro: Erica urceolata, also known as the Heaths or Heather, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to South Africa, belonging to the Ericaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Erica urceolata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
